markdown:
**TL;DR** — Apple filed a trade-secret lawsuit against OpenAI on July 10 over consumer hardware IP allegedly taken by two former engineers now at Jony Ive's io Products, while the ChatGPT integration partnership is officially declared unrelated — a distinction that may not hold long.

## Anthropic and Claude

- **Claude for Teachers launched July 14** — free for verified U.S. K-12 educators, covers lesson planning tied to all 50 states' standards, integrates with nine ed-tech platforms (ASSISTments, Brisk, Canva Education, etc.), and explicitly opts out of model training under a FERPA-compliant DPA. [source](https://www.anthropic.com/news)
- **Claude Code 2.1.208–2.1.210 all shipped July 14** — highlights: screen reader mode (`--ax-screen-reader` / `CLAUDE_AX_SCREEN_READER=1`), `vimInsertModeRemaps` for sequences like `jj`→Escape, a live elapsed-time counter on collapsed tool lines, and a 37% streaming CPU reduction; critical fix in 2.1.210 for `isolation: 'worktree'` subagents that were running git-mutating commands against the main repo instead of the worktree. [source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Fast mode for Claude Opus 4.7 deprecated** — removal date July 24; any call with `speed: "fast"` will error after that. [source](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/release-notes/api) *(redirected from docs.anthropic.com)*
- **"Inviting Hard Questions" initiative** (July 9) — Anthropic is soliciting the public's hardest AI questions and committing to publish its reasoning publicly; a transparency gesture worth bookmarking. [source](https://www.anthropic.com/news)
- **$10M committed to Canadian AI research; Ben Bernanke joins Long-Term Benefit Trust** — both announced this week, institutional relationship-building rather than product news. [source](https://www.anthropic.com/news)

## NixOS ecosystem

- **NixOS documentation team seeking renewed funding** (July 10) — the Foundation is actively soliciting support after acknowledging that "newcomers frequently struggle with our documentation, get frustrated and notice gaps"; if you rely on nixos.org docs, this is the ask to watch. [source](https://nixos.org/blog/)
- **NixOS 26.05 "Yarara" (ongoing since May 30)** — GNOME 50 "Tokyo", GCC 15, 17,532 packages removed from nixpkgs; critically, **this is the last release supporting x86_64-darwin** — Intel Mac binaries end with 26.11. [source](https://nixos.org/blog/announcements/2026/nixos-2605/)
- **Active `nix flake update` regression** — nixpkgs issue #426332 documents intermittent `nixos-rebuild` breakage after running `nix flake update` on a system flake; no fix merged yet, check the thread before debugging your config. [source](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues/426332) *(outside approved set)*

## AI coding agents and agent frameworks

- **Apple sued OpenAI over consumer hardware trade secrets** (July 10) — defendants include Chang Liu and Tang Tan (former Apple engineers now at io Products); Jony Ive is notably not named despite founding io. The complaint explicitly carves out the ChatGPT integration partnership as unrelated. [source](https://daringfireball.net/)
- **OpenAI's "super app" rollout was a mess** (July 9) — unified desktop combining ChatGPT, Work mode, and Codex ships as a 1.5GB Electron bundle versus the old native app's 159MB; the original app is now called "ChatGPT Classic," creating immediate brand confusion. Fidji Simo, who drove the pivot, departed the same day. [source](https://daringfireball.net/)
- **MCP + A2A now standard across all major frameworks** — LangGraph, Claude Agent SDK, OpenAI Agents SDK, Google ADK, CrewAI, AutoGen, and Pydantic AI all support both protocols; durable state and human-in-the-loop are now first-class primitives rather than afterthoughts. *(outside approved set — framework survey roundups)*

## Self-hosting and homelab

- **jscrambler npm 8.14.0 is malicious** (July 11) — the package drops and executes an infostealer binary; if jscrambler is anywhere in a build pipeline, audit immediately. *(outside approved set)*
- **Progress Software ShareFile emergency advisory** (July 10) — customers told to shut down Windows servers running Storage Zone Controllers in response to a "credible external security threat." *(outside approved set)*
- **XQUIC HTTP/3 crash, no patch** (disclosed July 8) — a flaw in Alibaba's XQUIC library lets any remote client crash an HTTP/3 server; relevant if you're running HTTP/3 on any self-hosted stack. *(outside approved set)*
- **Immich v3 keeps its rapid cadence; SigNoz pushed a major dashboard overhaul; Keycloak added a preview SCIM API** — all shipping this past week per the Elestio update catalog. *(outside approved set)*

## Local and on-device LLM inference

- **Ollama raised a $65M Series B** (July 9, led by Theory Ventures) — 8.9M developers, used inside 85% of Fortune 500, 14 employees, total funding now $88M; this confirms Ollama as the default open-model runtime layer rather than just a hobbyist tool. [source](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/09/popular-open-source-ai-developer-tool-ollama-raises-65m-grows-to-nearly-9m-users/) *(outside approved set)*
- **Ollama's MLX backend is now stable on Apple Silicon** — replaced the llama.cpp Metal path; M5 Max running Qwen3.5-35B shows +57% prefill and +93% decode throughput; this is the biggest local perf story of the month for Mac homelabbers. [source](https://ollama.com/blog/mlx) *(outside approved set)*
- **ExecuTorch 1.0 GA (ongoing since Oct 2025)** — Meta's on-device runtime ships with a 50KB base, 12+ hardware backends (Apple, Qualcomm, Arm, MediaTek, Vulkan), and 80%+ of popular edge LLMs on HuggingFace running out of the box; the production-readiness milestone is still underappreciated. *(outside approved set)*
